# Node Signals

> Signal emission and connection for event-driven programming

**Category:** Ui · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** ui · godot

## What this does

Signal emission and connection for event-driven programming

Specifies 4 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## What must be true

- **implementation:** Feature implemented in C++ engine core
- **platform_agnostic:** Works across desktop, web, mobile platforms

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Signal Connection** — when Connect signals to methods is requested, then Connect signals to methods completed.
- **Signal Emission** — when Emit signals with payloads is requested, then Emit signals with payloads completed.
- **Signal Disconnection** — when Remove signal connections is requested, then Remove signal connections completed.
- **Custom Signals** — when Define app-specific signals is requested, then Define app-specific signals completed.
